metadata.description: Aggregate demographic data on open-source contributors from published surveys and research
sources: Tidelift State of the Open Source Maintainer 2024
Vasilescu et al. 2015 (Gender and Tenure in GitHub)
Terrell et al. 2017 (Gender Differences in PRs)
metadata.caveats: All demographic data has significant limitations. Survey data is self-selected. Name-based gender inference has ~70-85% accuracy and is binary/Western-biased. Geographic data from profiles is self-reported (~60-70% fill rate).

gender_distribution.notes: Self-reported. Survey has known selection bias toward English-speaking, professional developers.
gender_distribution.notes_on_trend: Essentially flat since 2020. The 2018-2019 higher numbers may reflect survey methodology changes, not real gains. NOTE: 2023 survey DROPPED the gender question entirely — gap in data. Gender percentages are self-reported and rendered in interactive JS charts, not easily verifiable via scraping. Recommend checking downloadable CSV from survey.stackoverflow.co.

geographic_distribution.notes: IMPORTANT UPDATE: Octoverse 2025 (Sep 2024–Aug 2025) reports India OVERTOOK the US as largest open-source contributor base. India added 5.2M developers (14% of all new GitHub accounts). 180M+ total developers on GitHub. Growth: ~25 devs/min from APAC, ~12 from Europe, ~6.5 from Africa/ME, ~6 from LATAM. Country-level % breakdown needs updating from 2025 report.

linux_kernel.notes: Most kernel developers are employed by Intel, Red Hat, Google, Samsung, etc.

general_oss_maintainers.notes: CORRECTED: Tidelift 2024 says 60% describe themselves as unpaid hobbyists (not 74%). 47% say they receive no payment at all. Previous '74%' figure was wrong.

corporate_vs_volunteer.key_finding: Critical infrastructure is funded. Everything else is volunteer labor.

pr_acceptance_research.source: Terrell et al. 2017, PeerJ Computer Science (3:e111)
pr_acceptance_research.finding_corrected: Women's PRs accepted at 78.6% OVERALL (vs 74.6% for men). For OUTSIDERS specifically: women's acceptance is 71.8% with gender-neutral profiles, dropping to 62.5% when gender is identifiable. Men outsiders: relatively stable.
pr_acceptance_research.notes: CORRECTED: 78.6% is the OVERALL acceptance rate for women, not specifically 'when hidden.' The gender-neutral outsider rate is 71.8%. The 62.5% drop applies to outsiders when gender is identifiable. Previous framing was imprecise.

mobile_os_market_share.insight: Android (open source) dominates where incomes are lower. iOS dominates in wealthy countries. Open source literally provides mobile computing to billions who couldn't afford proprietary alternatives.

maintainer_burnout.notes: CORRECTED: 60% quit or considered quitting (CONFIRMED). 46% harassment figure TRACED: it conflates '45% witnessed rudeness' from the 2017 GitHub Open Source Survey (5,500+ respondents). The actual figures: 45% witnessed rudeness, 18% personally experienced a negative interaction, 21% stopped contributing after witnessing/experiencing negativity. Source: opensourcesurvey.org/2017.
